req.title: Joint account operations (boundary variant)
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
preconditions: holder is joint
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-043 defines joint-account behaviour across three axes: (1) a primary holder can perform transactions (positive/EP), (2) a secondary holder is forbidden from modifying ownership (negative/NEG+DT), and (3) boundary thresholds on transaction amounts must be exercised at exact min/max values (boundary/BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition where a secondary holder attempts a legitimate read then an illegal write, verifying the state machine rejects the write while permitting the read (STC). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down the joint account.

tests.name: test_primary_holder_can_perform_transaction
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder
Primary holder auth token is valid
Account balance is sufficient for the transaction
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with a valid transaction payload (amount within allowed range)
expected: Response body confirms transaction was applied
trace: R-FIN-043: 'A joint-account holder shall perform transactions'

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_cannot_modify_ownership
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder
Secondary holder auth token is valid
No pending ownership-change requests exist for this account
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/joint with payload requesting ownership transfer to secondary holder
expected: Response body contains an error indicating insufficient privileges
trace: R-FIN-043: 'the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'
Error pathway: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

tests.name: test_transaction_at_minimum_threshold
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary holder
Primary holder auth token is valid
Minimum allowed transaction amount is defined (e.g. 0.01)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with amount equal to the exact minimum threshold (0.01)
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 is returned for amount == MIN_AMOUNT
Transaction is applied and reflected in the response
trace: R-FIN-043: 'Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested'
BVA: exact lower boundary of transaction amount

tests.name: test_transaction_below_minimum_threshold_rejected
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary holder
Primary holder auth token is valid
Minimum allowed transaction amount is 0.01; amounts below it are invalid
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with amount equal to MIN_AMOUNT - 0.01 (i.e. 0.00)
expected: Response body describes the amount validation failure
trace: R-FIN-043: 'Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested'
BVA: one step below lower boundary (off-by-one)

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_read_then_write_state_transition
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder
Secondary holder auth token is valid
Account is in an active state
actions: GET /accounts/{id}/joint to confirm read is permitted (account in READABLE state for secondary)
PATCH /accounts/{id}/joint with ownership-change payload to trigger forbidden write transition
expected: GET returns HTTP 200 with account details (read state is allowed)
PATCH returns HTTP 403 (write/ownership-change state transition is blocked)
trace: R-FIN-043: secondary holder read is permitted; ownership modification is not
STC: READABLE -> FORBIDDEN_WRITE transition for secondary holder role

tests.name: test_transaction_at_maximum_threshold
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary holder
Primary holder auth token is valid
Account balance is >= MAX_AMOUNT
Maximum single-transaction amount is defined (e.g. 1000000.00)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with amount equal to the exact maximum threshold (1000000.00)
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 is returned for amount == MAX_AMOUNT
Transaction is applied and reflected in the response
trace: R-FIN-043: 'Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested'
BVA: exact upper boundary of transaction amount

tests.name: test_decision_table_all_holder_role_and_operation_combinations
preconditions: A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder
Both primary and secondary auth tokens are valid
actions: For each (role, operation) pair in the decision table, perform the corresponding API call
Verify the expected HTTP status code per rule
trace: R-FIN-043: primary holder may transact AND modify ownership; secondary holder may only transact
DT: all combinations of {primary, secondary} x {transaction, ownership_change}

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```json
{
  "requirement_id": "R-FIN-043",
  "reasoning": "R-FIN-043 defines joint-account behaviour across three axes: (1) a primary holder can perform transactions (positive/EP), (2) a secondary holder is forbidden from modifying ownership (negative/NEG+DT), and (3) boundary thresholds on transaction amounts must be exercised at exact min/max values (boundary/BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition where a secondary holder attempts a legitimate read then an illegal write, verifying the state machine rejects the write while permitting the read (STC). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down the joint account.",
  "tests": [
    {
      "name": "test_primary_holder_can_perform_transaction",
      "kind": "positive",
      "heuristic": "EP",
      "preconditions": [
        "A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder",
        "Primary holder auth token is valid",
        "Account balance is sufficient for the transaction"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "Authenticate as the primary holder",
        "POST /accounts/{id}/joint with a valid transaction payload (amount within allowed range)"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "HTTP 200 or 201 is returned",
        "Response body confirms transaction was applied",
        "Account balance reflects the transaction"
      ],
      "trace": [
        "R-FIN-043: 'A joint-account holder shall perform transactions'",
        "Actor: customer (primary holder)",
        "Expected outcome: operation applied"
      ],
      "executable": "import pytest\n\ndef test_primary_holder_can_perform_transaction(joint_account, primary_auth_token, api_client):\n    account_id = joint_account[\"id\"]\n    payload = {\"type\": \"deposit\", \"amount\": 500.00, \"currency\": \"USD\"}\n    headers = {\"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {primary_auth_token}\"}\n\n    resp = api_client.post(\n        f\"/accounts/{account_id}/joint\",\n        json=payload,\n        headers=headers\n    )\n\n    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (\n        f\"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}\"\n    )\n    body = resp.json()\n    assert body.get(\"status\") in (\"applied\", \"completed\", \"success\"), (\n        f\"Unexpected status in body: {body}\"\n    )\n    assert \"transaction_id\" in body, \"transaction_id missing from response\"\n"
    },
    {
      "name": "test_secondary_holder_cannot_modify_ownership",
      "kind": "negative",
      "heuristic": "NEG",
      "preconditions": [
        "A joint account exists with a primary and secondary holder",
        "Secondary holder auth token is valid",
        "No pending ownership-change requests exist for this account"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "Authenticate as the secondary holder",
        "PATCH /accounts/{id}/joint with payload requesting ownership transfer to secondary holder"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "HTTP 403 Forbidden is returned",
        "Response body contains an error indicating insufficient privileges",
        "Account ownership remains unchanged"
      ],
      "trace": [
        "R-FIN-043: 'the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'",
        "Error pathway: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change"
      ],
      "executable": "import pytest\n\ndef test_secondary_holder_cannot_modify_ownership(joint_account, secondary_auth_token, api_client):\n    account_id = joint_account[\"id\"]\n    payload = {\"action\": \"transfer_ownership\", \"new_primary\": joint_account[\"secondary_holder_id\"]}\n    headers = {\"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {secondary_auth_token}\"}\n\n    resp = api_client.patch(\n        f\"/accounts/{account_id}/joint\",\n        json=payload,\n        headers=headers\n    )\n\n    assert resp.status_code == 403, (\n        f\"Expected 403 Forbidden, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}\"\n    )\n    body = resp.json()\n    assert \"error\" in body or \"detail\" in body, \"Error descriptor missing from 403 body\"\n
